title: Eiffel Tower
description: Gustave Eiffel's iconic, wrought-iron 1889 tower, with steps and elevators to observation decks.

user_reviews.Name: Aysha Afrin
user_reviews.Description: What an amazing experience! Completely fell in love with the charm it has. Took the ticket from the site and went up the top of effiel. It was totally worth it. Took stairs to 2nd floor and then elevator to the top, the best choice to experience the complete tower. Has good cafes and gift shops and really loved the climb. When visiting during winter, make sure to wear something that keep you warm because it freezes on top due to the cold winds. A must try and Visit spot. I experienced the effiel tower from all corners, and from different angles around the city. Take the train for the view, get the view when it sparkles, take the scene cruise for a different experience and view

user_reviews.Name: Dry Gin
user_reviews.Description:
Visited the Eiffel Tower with an e-ticket and had a great experience. I would highly recommend purchasing your tickets online in advance to avoid disappointment, as tickets can sell out quickly.
The first security check does not require you to show your ticket. After that, proceed to the “E-Tickets – Elevator” entrance with the green signboard. Visitors are only allowed to enter during their designated time slot, so there is no need to arrive too early. After entering, there is a second security check where your ticket will be scanned. The elevator ride to the 2nd floor takes about a minute. If you are visiting the summit, you will need to change to another elevator. Everything was very well organized and the crowd control was excellent. The staff did a great job managing the large number of visitors, making the whole process smooth and efficient. Overall, it was a fantastic experience and one of the highlights of our trip to Paris.

user_reviews.Name: Share Share
user_reviews.Description:
Eiffel Tower is the defining symbol of Paris and one of the world’s most recognizable landmarks. The views from the top are spectacular, especially at sunset when the city glows in golden light and landmarks like the Seine, Arc de Triomphe, and Sacré-Cœur come into view. The tower itself is impressive up close, with its intricate ironwork and grand scale making it even more stunning in person.

The atmosphere around the tower is lively day and night, with street performers, gardens, and cafés nearby creating a classic Parisian experience. At night, the sparkling light show every hour adds a magical touch that makes the visit unforgettable. Booking tickets in advance is highly recommended to avoid long queues, especially during peak travel seasons.

Whether you visit for the panoramic views, photography, or simply to experience one of the world’s greatest landmarks, the Eiffel Tower is absolutely worth seeing at least once.

user_reviews.Name: Periha M
user_reviews.Description: The most beautiful and romantic place to see in Paris. Watching the golden glow of the tower at night and its sparkling lights is truly magical. Sitting on the grass of Champ de Mars and watching the tower was one of the best parts of my trip. Even if you don't go inside, seeing the area around it is totally worth it. 100% recommended!

user_reviews.Name: Va Zub
user_reviews.Description:
Absolutely a must-visit, especially at night. The view from the top is incredible, you can see the entire city glowing with lights and it creates a really unique atmosphere. When it lights up after sunset, the whole experience feels completely different and much more impressive than during the day.

Everything is well organized, the process is smooth, and the staff is helpful. Going up is definitely worth it, the perspective from the top is something you can’t fully capture in photos. It’s one of those places where you just stop and enjoy the moment. If you’re in the city, make sure to visit in the evening, it’s truly unforgettable and easily one of the highlights of the trip.

user_reviews.Name: O T
user_reviews.Description:
The Eiffel Tower was an unforgettable experience and one of the most memorable highlights of our trip to Paris! Seeing this iconic landmark in person was truly breathtaking. The views over the city are spectacular, offering a unique perspective of Paris’s beautiful architecture, gardens, and famous landmarks.

The experience was especially magical for our family. Our children were excited from the moment they saw the tower and were amazed by its size, history, and incredible views. Watching Paris from above created memories that we will cherish for years to come.💕

user_reviews.Name: Raghunadharao Digumarti
user_reviews.Description:
The long wait wa worth it - nearly an hour and a half - 2 security checks and a queue to board the elevators.

A masterpiece of human engineering that is still flawlessly running after a century, the steel tower is as fabulous as the views of Paris from its various decks.

Eiffel's office, with Thomas Edison and his phonograph thrown in, is a real treat.

The Eiffel Tour is definitely the most important of Parisian tourist sites.

The esplande and gardens are enticing.

user_reviews.Name: A Oki
user_reviews.Description:
It doesn’t feel like a real trip to Paris if you don’t visit the Eiffel Tower. It’s truly one of the world’s most iconic architectural landmarks and something everyone should see at least once in their lifetime.

The tower is beautiful both during the day and at night. In the daytime, you can really admire its structure and design. it’s amazing to think how engineers were able to build something like this so long ago. At night, the atmosphere completely changes, especially when the tower starts sparkling. It feels magical.

We decided to go up inside the tower, and it was a totally different experience seeing the Eiffel Tower from above. You get an incredible 360-degree view of Paris. If you’re afraid of heights or don’t want to climb the stairs, don’t worry, there’s a large elevator that takes many people at once.

An unforgettable experience and absolutely a must-visit in Paris.

title: Louvre Museum
description: Former historic palace housing huge art collection, from Roman sculptures to da Vinci's "Mona Lisa."

user_reviews.Name: MIKE N
user_reviews.Description:
First time visiting Paris, and the Louvre Museum was a must-see. The museum is absolutely massive, with incredible art and history around every corner. Seeing the Mona Lisa in person was a memorable experience.

Be prepared for large crowds, especially in June. The lines can be long, and many of the popular exhibits are packed with visitors. Buying tickets in advance is highly recommended to save time.

Even with the crowds, the Louvre is one of those places you have to visit at least once when in Paris. Plan several hours for your visit, wear comfortable shoes, and enjoy one of the world’s greatest museums.
user_reviews.text_original:

user_reviews.Name: Roushan Srivastav
user_reviews.Description:
An unforgettable, bucket-list experience in Paris!

Visiting the Louvre was an absolute highlight of my trip to Paris! The sheer scale of the museum is mind-blowing, and the building itself a stunning former royal palace is just as much a masterpiece as the art inside. Looking up at the incredibly ornate, gilded ceilings and walking through the grand galleries felt surreal.

Of course, seeing Leonardo da Vinci’s Mona Lisa in person was an unforgettable moment. Yes, the room is busy, but the line moves quickly and it is completely worth it. Beyond that, the Winged Victory of Samothrace standing tall at the top of the grand staircase and the beautiful Venus de Milo were absolutely breathtaking.

My top tips for anyone visiting:

1. Book your tickets online well in advance to secure your time slot and bypass the massive ticket lines.

2. Wear your most comfortable walking shoes because the museum is massive and you will easily clock in thousands of steps.

3. Pick a few must-see pieces before you go, or you will get easily overwhelmed

It is an absolute 5-star experience that you cannot miss when in Paris. Truly a journey through history and art!

user_reviews.Name: Guido Bertram
user_reviews.Description: The Louvre is not only an incredible museum but also a very large and impressive area to explore. Even outside the museum itself, you can easily spend half a day walking around the beautiful courtyards, the famous glass pyramid, and the surrounding architecture. The entire complex is stunning and offers many great spots for photos and to simply enjoy the atmosphere of Paris. A must-visit place, even if you don’t plan to go inside the museum.

user_reviews.Name: Akshay
user_reviews.Description:
Are you even in Louvre if you don’t get lost every 5 minutes. Amazing piece of history and helps us learn history.

There are 3 main wings. See where your favorite exhibits are and keep a note of it. If you wanna make good use of the time.

Even 8 hours in the museum wouldnt be enough to see everything you wanna see. Get ready to walk a lot.

Top picks: Select paintings and exhibits you want to see and book a ticket well in advance. Also reach the place atleast 30 minutes before your admit time.

Enjoy a piece of history and a must do in your first visit to Paris.

user_reviews.Name: O T
user_reviews.Description:
The museum is absolutely breathtaking, with stunning architecture, beautiful galleries, and an incredible collection of art from around the world. From the iconic Mona Lisa to the magnificent sculptures and historical treasures, every room offered something fascinating to discover.

What made the experience even more special was seeing it through the eyes of our young children. Our minor children were captivated by the grand halls, impressive sculptures, and famous masterpieces. The museum offered countless opportunities for curiosity, learning, and family discussions about art, history, and culture.

We spent hours exploring and still felt there was more to see. The Louvre is much more than a museum—it is a journey through human creativity, culture, and history. An unforgettable family experience that inspired both adults and children alike. Highly recommended for visitors of all ages and a wonderful destination for families traveling with children!

user_reviews.Name: Laidback Jay
user_reviews.Description: What an amazing experience! I visited the Louvre in May and was blown away. This museum is absolutely HUGE and can easily take a couple of hours, if not more, to explore fully. We only waited 10-30 minutes, which was fantastic, but definitely get your tickets in advance! Just a heads-up, be aware of the numerous scammers roaming the grounds outside!

user_reviews.Name: C Lo
user_reviews.Description:
Pretty big museum with lots of visitors. A little crowded for my taste but liked seeing the impressive and massive collection of paintings and history in the paintings. Also, liked the Egyptian mummies.

They had sections that were closed off which I was disappointed in. I think they should announce which sections are closed at the entrance and/ or on their website.  There are basically 3 sections but parts of these sections were closed off.

In addition, as a suggestion for improvement, they need more toilets for the millions and amounts of guests in their complementary museum restrooms. Long lines…

Also, even with a timed ticket and getting there 30 min earlier, still had to wait about 30-60 min to get in… think it should be more efficient. It wasn’t that we were moving even a little. Need some electronic faster scanning system to get people in and out more efficiently.

user_reviews.Name: Travel Reviews
user_reviews.Description: Amazing museum with plenty of things to see and explore. I definitely recommend you to visit this place if you are in Paris. Make sure you buy tickets in advance and that you have tolhe option of “skip the line” with thr guide or otherwise you might end up in the line for several hours. Even with “skip the line” option we had to wait for 45min to enter the museum.  Sightseeing with the guide was a big plus for us.
